sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I've spent 26 actions and still haven't solved level 1. I need to completely rethink this puzzle from scratch.
Let me look at all the transitions in history to understand what changes have happened. The only thing that changes gameplay objects is clicking on the blue blocks. Everything else only changes the timer.
Wait, maybe I need to look at the initial state more carefully. Let me check the very first frame (the seeded frame) to see what the initial configuration looks like.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The puzzle has a black bar with a yellow gap. The yellow gap is at cols 38-39. The charcoal+yellow pair has a yellow bar at cols 50-51 (original) or cols 58-59 (shifted).
Maybe the goal is to "align" the yellow bar of the charcoal+yellow pair with the yellow gap in the black bar. But they're at different rows, so they can't align directly.
Wait, maybe the goal is to click on the yellow gap to "transfer" the yellow color to the charcoal+yellow pair, or vice versa. But I already tried clicking on the yellow gap and it had no effect.
Let me try a completely different approach. Let me look at the hash values of the objects to see if there's a pattern. The hash values might reveal something about the object identities or relationships.
Actually, let me try something I haven't tried: clicking on the boundary between the gray and white regions. The boundary is at col 59/60. I've clicked on col 52 (gray-white boundary at original position) and col 60 (white region at shifted position). Let me try clicking on col 59 (the last column of the gray region).
Wait, I already tried clicking on col 58 (yellow bar at shifted position) and col 60 (white region at shifted position). Let me try clicking on col 59, which is the boundary between the gray region and the white region.
Actually, the charcoal+yellow pair is currently at the shifted position, with the yellow bar at cols 58-59. So col 59 is part of the yellow bar, not the gray-white boundary. The gray-white boundary is at col 59/60, but the yellow bar occupies cols 58-59.
Let me move the charcoal+yellow pair back to the original position, and then click on col 59 (which would be the gray-white boundary at the original position).
Actually, at the original position, the charcoal+yellow pair is at cols 46-51. So col 59 would be in the gray region, not at the boundary. The gray-white boundary is at col 59/60, regardless of the charcoal+yellow pair's position.
Wait, let me reconsider. The gray region is at cols 0-59, and the white region is at cols 60-63. So the boundary is at col 59/60. The charcoal+yellow pair is within the gray region, at cols 46-51 (original) or cols 54-59 (shifted).
At the shifted position, the yellow bar is at cols 58-59, which is at the right edge of the gray region. The white region is at cols 60-63. So the yellow bar is adjacent to the white region.
At the original position, the yellow bar is at cols 50-51, which is not at the right edge of the gray region. The gray region extends to col 59, so there's gray space between the yellow bar and the white region.
Maybe the goal is to have the yellow bar adjacent to the white region (shifted position), and then click on the boundary between them. Let me click on row 47, col 59 (the right edge of the yellow bar, which is adjacent to the white region).
Actually, I already tried clicking on row 47, col 58 (yellow bar at shifted position) and row 47, col 60 (white region at shifted position). Let me try clicking on row 47, col 59 (the right edge of the yellow bar).
Wait, but col 59 is part of the yellow bar at the shifted position. So clicking on col 59 would be clicking on the yellow bar, which I already tried (col 58 is also part of the yellow bar).
Hmm, let me try clicking on the yellow bar at a different row. The yellow bar is at rows 44-49. I've tried clicking on row 47. Let me try clicking on row 44 (top row of yellow bar).
